Friction complaint about targeted-bounty pay/access, 2026-07-08. 2026-07-17: follow-up post, same theme — asking to be made eligible for targeted bounties and for solvable (not decades-unsolved) problems to be posted. Recurring, unresolved usability friction, not a new signal class.

3 posts 2026-07-07 explaining Verdikta bounty flow to CN Web3 audience, organic evangelism (previously flagged as possibly scripted by mention-radar). 2026-07-14: 3 more posts, now substantive not templated — quotes/links a Medium deep-dive (by s97472091, the tracked builder) analyzing bounty #40's 99/100 score, and shares s97472091-pixel's new verdikta-playbook site — amplifying the top building lead, not just self-narrating.

PR #18 to verdikta-applications ('fix: align bounty submission scripts with ETH prepay') — fixed real ABI/flow bugs in the onboarding scripts (ethMaxBudget, payable startPreparedSubmission, stuck-submission diagnostics), 796/797 lines across 2 files, node --check + validate_contract_config.js testing evidence, closes issue #17 (nigelon11). leads.who: NSPG13/agent-bounties — "Agent Bounties" (competitor, not a builder-on-Verdikta lead)
leads.signal: Open-source AI-agent bounty protocol on Base (post/fund/claim/solve/verify/pay flow, MCP tools, x402, agent wallets) — same wedge as Verdikta. Repo created 2026-07-08, shipping fast (288 merged PRs by 2026-07-16, several/day). docs/competitor-learning-plan.md contains a detailed writeup of Verdikta's design pulled from the agents API, bounty app, and whitepaper (bounties.verdikta.org/agents, verdikta.org/whitepaper) with explicit "avoid" lessons (mandatory API keys, solver-paid evaluation, unbounded AI authority, consensus-without-appeal). This is competitive intelligence, not a partnership candidate — flagging for operator awareness, not outreach.

2026-07-16: unprompted reply pitching Verdikta's AI-jury evaluation layer as a drop-in scoring backend for a third party's bounty/DAO Discord bot governance repo — "that part's already solved... could plug into the bot via API instead of building scoring logic from scratch," offered to share docs. Organic third-party advocacy, not self-promotion of their own bounty activity. Note: bio references "Zeta_v1" which is superficially similar to the unrelated `Zetav1` GitHub bounty-farming spam cluster (fake Canonical-Wallet PRs against verdikta-docs/verdikta-dispatcher) — checked, no other overlap (different platform, different behavior pattern, tweet content is substantive not templated); treating as coincidental naming, not the same actor. Low engagement (0 likes, 53 views). 2026-07-17: second, distinct unprompted pitch — commented on an "agoragentic-integrations" adapter-catalog repo (11 free wrappers, scaling past 50) suggesting Verdikta's AI-jury to keep the catalog verified as it grows. Two organic pitches to two different third-party repos within 2 days — a repeat pattern, not a one-off.
